title: Flaky Light Jamaican Parmigiano-Reggiano Fritters
description: Fresh, flaky, and light Jamaican dish featuring Parmigiano-Reggiano, perfect for a snack or dessert.

ingredients.name: Scallop Shells
ingredients.notes: Cleaned, dried, and reserved for fritter formation

ingredients.name: Jamaican Jerk Spice
ingredients.notes: Commercial blend or homemade, omit for a milder taste

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Flaky sea salt, or table salt as a substitute

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: Beaten, for egg wash on fritters

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Melted, for brushing pastry before baking

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Ice-cold, for egg wash and pastry hydration

instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Roll out the laminated puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut the pastry into 12 equal triangles, reserving any scraps for trimming.
Brush the pastry triangles with the melted butter and sprinkle with grated Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese.
Dust the edges with confectioners' sugar, if using, and fold the triangles into triangles.
Place the fritters on the prepared baking sheet, leaving about 1 inch of space between each.
Bake the fritters in the preheated oven for 18-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the fritters are baking, prepare the Jamaican jerk spice mixture by blending 1 teaspoon of jerk spice with 1/4 teaspoon of salt.
Once the fritters are done, brush them with the beaten egg for a golden glaze and sprinkle with toasted coconut flakes.
Serve the fritters warm, garnished with a sprinkle of jerk spice mixture and a side of your favorite dipping sauce.
